content: Autonomous curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents (from Topic A's ICM mechanism framework) can be used to actively prune and prioritize long-term memory storage for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents, solving the critical scaling problem of unbounded memory growth in open-ended active learning. This creates a self-sustaining memory cycle where an agent's innate curiosity signals which new experiences are worth retaining, rather than relying on manual memory retention rules.
